root_motivation: Make fabricated or ambiguous MQO references a structural hard-error rather than a silently-wrong name in generated SQL, by turning R1–R3 from prompt rules into a deterministic code gate that the MQO compiler must pass before it can emit any query.
user_persona: An MQO compiler pipeline (mqo-mcp-server or similar automation), dispatched by an LLM, that needs a validated BoundMqo before calling the SQL generator.  Also: an AtScale engineer or SE running one-off verification of a catalog snapshot against a draft MQO.

acceptance_criteria.description: A valid MQO over a fixture catalog binds every measure, level, and filter ref to its unique_name and exits 0. Binding is case-insensitive against both unique_name and label.
A fabricated measure or level name not in the catalog produces a {"not_found":[...]} report and exit 4. The binder must never guess or substitute a close match.
An ambiguous label (same label in two or more columns) produces a {"ambiguous":[{ref, candidates:[...]}]} report and exit 3 with the full candidate set.

A CalcGroupMember filter is resolved exclusively from the Calculation Groups section of describe_model output; its MDX is carried into the BoundMqo. A member not found in that section is reported not_found, never guessed.

A semi-additive measure is flagged semi_additive=true in the BoundMqo output and carries the full trigger_hierarchies list; a non-semi-additive measure has semi_additive=false and empty trigger_hierarchies.
cargo test --release passes with zero failures; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings emits no diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria.test: cargo test --release 2>&1 | grep -c 'FAILED' | grep -q '^0$' && cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings 2>&1 | grep -cE 'error|warning\[' | grep -q '^0$'

scope: CLI binary mqo-bind: reads --mqo <file> and --catalog <file>, resolves all MQO refs, emits BoundMqo or error report as JSON on stdout.
Resolution modes: exact unique_name match (case-insensitive) then label match (case-insensitive).
Structured not_found and ambiguous error reports, each listing all unresolved/colliding refs in a single pass.
CalcGroupMember resolution from describe_model calc_groups section only.
Semi-additive flag and trigger_hierarchies forwarded from catalog metadata.
required_dimension forwarded from catalog metadata for R11 compiler enforcement.
Exit codes: 0 bound, 3 ambiguous, 4 not-found, 2 bad input.
Dependency on mqo-spec git crate for MQO/BoundMqo types.
non_goals: SQL compilation or query execution — binder stops at unique_name resolution.
Fuzzy matching / Levenshtein suggestions — not_found is a hard stop, no guessing.
Catalog snapshot retrieval (no network calls, no MCP calls) — snapshot must be pre-fetched and passed as --catalog.
Multi-model disambiguation when both models are equally valid — caller must disambiguate by prefixing unique_name.
Updating or mutating the catalog snapshot — the snapshot is read-only.
WASM, async, or service target — CLI only.
Building a full BoundMqo type inside this crate — uses extended local types (BoundMqoOutput, BoundMeasureExt, etc.) that are a superset of what mqo_spec::BoundMqo currently exposes.

additional.forbid_unsafe_code: src/main.rs carries #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — must not be removed or weakened
additional.exit_codes_are_contract: Exit codes 0/2/3/4 are part of the public API; any change requires AC amendment

five_whys_trace.q: Why do you need a CLI that resolves MQO references against a catalog?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the MQO compiler currently trusts the LLM-supplied unique_names verbatim, and fabricated names silently produce wrong SQL.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the compiler trust the LLM without validation?
five_whys_trace.a: Because R1–R3 (identify exact unique_names, present alternatives on ambiguity, confirm with rejected alternatives) are prompt rules, not code gates — the LLM can violate them without any structural check.

five_whys_trace.q: Why are R1–R3 implemented as prompt rules instead of code?
five_whys_trace.a: Because when the MQO pipeline was designed, the catalog was queried inline by the agent; there was no pre-compiled catalog snapshot to validate against before compilation.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is a catalog snapshot now available?
five_whys_trace.a: mqo-mcp-server now calls list_models/search_columns/describe_model and stores the result as a JSON bundle before dispatching the compiler; that bundle is the ground truth the binder can validate against.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the binder guess close matches (Levenshtein) for not_found refs?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. AC2 explicitly says 'never a guessed substitute.' not_found is a hard stop.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What if both ambiguous and not_found conditions occur in one MQO?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Ambiguous takes precedence over not_found per the implementation comment in binder.rs: collect all, if any ambiguous → Ambiguous; else if any not_found → NotFound.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Does required_dimension belong in BoundMeasureExt or only in catalog metadata?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Carried into BoundMeasureExt so the downstream compiler can enforce R11 without re-reading the catalog.
